title: L6: For Loops
description: Use range() and for loops to repeat code a known number of times.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

5 minutes - from last lesson

Answer these quick questions from Selection Part 2 before learning for loops.

Range Check: Selection Part 2

Use if condition: for the first check, elif condition: for another check, and else: as the fallback.

Comparison operators include == for exactly equal, != for not equal, >= for at least, and <= for at most.

For combined conditions, use and when both checks must be True, and or when either check can be True.

steps.title: The Range Function
steps.content:
The Range Function

range() creates a sequence of numbers for a loop to use.

range(5)        # 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
range(1, 6)     # 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
range(0, 10, 2) # 0, 2, 4, 6, 8

The start is included. The stop is not included. The third number is the step size.

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on
steps.js:
if (window.PyLearn && PyLearn.createEditor) PyLearn.createEditor({ containerId: "for-range-demo", initialCode: "for i in range(1, 6):\n print(i)", persist: false }); (function(){var root=document.getElementById('range-intro-check');if(!root)return;var q={"prompt":"Write a range() call that produces 0, 1, 2, 3, 4.","pattern":"^\\s*range\\s*\\(\\s*(?:5|0\\s*,\\s*5(?:\\s*,\\s*1)?)\\s*\\)\\s*$","hint":"range(5) or range(0, 5)","wrong":"The stop value should be one after the final number."};function ok(v){v=String(v||'').trim().replace(/[“”]/g,'"').replace(/[‘’]/g,"'");return new RegExp(q.pattern,q.flags||'im').test(v)}function render(){root.innerHTML='
'+q.prompt+'
'+(q.rows>1?'':'')+'
';var input=root.querySelector(q.rows>1?'textarea':'input'),fb=root.querySelector('.ex-feedback'),exp=root.querySelector('.ex-explanation');root.querySelector('button').onclick=function(){if(!input.value.trim()){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Write your answer first.';return}if(ok(input.value)){input.classList.add('correct');fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Practice complete. You can move on.';if(q.hint){exp.textContent=q.hint;exp.style.display='block'}window.__markStepComplete&&window.__markStepComplete()}else{input.classList.add('wrong');fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent=q.wrong||'Not quite. Use the scaffold below.';if(q.hint){exp.textContent=q.hint;exp.style.display='block'}setTimeout(function(){input.classList.remove('wrong')},900)}};input.onkeydown=function(e){if(e.key==='Enter'&&q.rows<=1)root.querySelector('button').click();if(e.key==='Enter'&&e.ctrlKey)root.querySelector('button').click()};input.focus()}render()})();

steps.title: Step Through Range
steps.content:
Step Through Range

Click through each stage to see how Python decides which numbers are produced.

steps.title: Range Practice
steps.content:
Range Practice

Practise reading and writing range() calls.

steps.title: Writing a For Loop
steps.content:
Writing a For Loop

A for loop repeats code once for each value in a sequence.

for i in range(5):
    print(i)

i is the loop variable. The indented code is the code that repeats.

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on
steps.js:
if (window.PyLearn && PyLearn.createEditor) PyLearn.createEditor({ containerId: "for-line-demo", initialCode: "for i in range(5):\n print(i)", persist: false }); (function(){var root=document.getElementById('for-first-line-check');if(!root)return;var q={"prompt":"Write the first line of a for loop that repeats exactly 4 times.","pattern":"^\\s*for\\s+\\w+\\s+in\\s+range\\s*\\(\\s*(?:4|0\\s*,\\s*4(?:\\s*,\\s*1)?)\\s*\\)\\s*:\\s*$","hint":"for i in range(4):","wrong":"Use for, a loop variable, in, range(4), and a colon."};function ok(v){v=String(v||'').trim().replace(/[“”]/g,'"').replace(/[‘’]/g,"'");return new RegExp(q.pattern,q.flags||'im').test(v)}function render(){root.innerHTML='
'+q.prompt+'
'+(q.rows>1?'':'')+'
';var input=root.querySelector(q.rows>1?'textarea':'input'),fb=root.querySelector('.ex-feedback'),exp=root.querySelector('.ex-explanation');root.querySelector('button').onclick=function(){if(!input.value.trim()){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Write your answer first.';return}if(ok(input.value)){input.classList.add('correct');fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Practice complete. You can move on.';if(q.hint){exp.textContent=q.hint;exp.style.display='block'}window.__markStepComplete&&window.__markStepComplete()}else{input.classList.add('wrong');fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent=q.wrong||'Not quite. Use the scaffold below.';if(q.hint){exp.textContent=q.hint;exp.style.display='block'}setTimeout(function(){input.classList.remove('wrong')},900)}};input.onkeydown=function(e){if(e.key==='Enter'&&q.rows<=1)root.querySelector('button').click();if(e.key==='Enter'&&e.ctrlKey)root.querySelector('button').click()};input.focus()}render()})();

steps.title: For Loop First Lines
steps.content:
For Loop First Lines

Write the first line only. Remember the colon.

steps.title: For Loops With Print
steps.content:
For Loops With Print

To print each number, put print(i) on the next line and indent it.

for i in range(1, 4):
    print(i)

Click run to see what happens

Now practise this before moving on
steps.js:
if (window.PyLearn && PyLearn.createEditor) PyLearn.createEditor({ containerId: "for-print-demo", initialCode: "for i in range(1, 4):\n print(i)", persist: false }); (function(){var root=document.getElementById('for-print-check');if(!root)return;var q={"prompt":"Write a complete loop that prints 1, 2 and 3 on separate lines.","rows":3,"pattern":"^\\s*for\\s+(\\w+)\\s+in\\s+range\\s*\\(\\s*1\\s*,\\s*4(?:\\s*,\\s*1)?\\s*\\)\\s*:\\s*[\\r\\n]+\\s+print\\s*\\(\\s*\\1\\s*\\)\\s*$","hint":"for i in range(1, 4):\n print(i)","wrong":"Use range(1, 4), then indent print(i)."};function ok(v){v=String(v||'').trim().replace(/[“”]/g,'"').replace(/[‘’]/g,"'");return new RegExp(q.pattern,q.flags||'im').test(v)}function render(){root.innerHTML='
'+q.prompt+'
'+(q.rows>1?'':'')+'
';var input=root.querySelector(q.rows>1?'textarea':'input'),fb=root.querySelector('.ex-feedback'),exp=root.querySelector('.ex-explanation');root.querySelector('button').onclick=function(){if(!input.value.trim()){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Write your answer first.';return}if(ok(input.value)){input.classList.add('correct');fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Practice complete. You can move on.';if(q.hint){exp.textContent=q.hint;exp.style.display='block'}window.__markStepComplete&&window.__markStepComplete()}else{input.classList.add('wrong');fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent=q.wrong||'Not quite. Use the scaffold below.';if(q.hint){exp.textContent=q.hint;exp.style.display='block'}setTimeout(function(){input.classList.remove('wrong')},900)}};input.onkeydown=function(e){if(e.key==='Enter'&&q.rows<=1)root.querySelector('button').click();if(e.key==='Enter'&&e.ctrlKey)root.querySelector('button').click()};input.focus()}render()})();

steps.title: For Loops With Print Practice
steps.content:
For Loops With Print Practice

Write complete loops. Use a new indented line for the code inside the loop.

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
Exam 6: Range & For Loops

Work through all questions. This checks range(), for loop first lines, and printing values inside a loop.

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless Practice

Random questions from across the For Loops lesson. Answer 5 questions correctly to complete this step.

quizQuestions.q: Which first line repeats exactly 4 times?
What does the third value in range(2, 10, 2) control?
Write a range() call that produces 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.
Write the first line of a for loop where i goes from 1 to 10. Include the colon.
Write a complete for loop that prints the numbers 1 to 5 on separate lines.
Write a complete for loop that prints the even numbers 0, 2, 4, 6, 8 on separate lines.
Which loop prints Hello exactly three times?
Write a countdown for loop that prints 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 on separate lines.
Write a complete for loop that prints 10 to 15 inclusive.
Why is the print line indented in a for loop?
options: So it runs inside the loop
So it runs before the loop
So range includes the stop value

quizQuestions.q: Write a for loop that prints Python exactly four times.
Write a for loop that prints the 3 times table from 3 to 15.
When should a for loop usually be chosen?
options: When the number of repeats is known
Only when a condition is False
When the code must never stop
